## CV & Portfolio Project - Tailwind CSS

This is a professional CV/portfolio website built with HTML and Tailwind CSS.

## Project Setup Complete ✓

All steps have been completed:

1. ✓ Project scaffolding - HTML with Tailwind CSS CDN
2. ✓ Custom styling - Blue theme with responsive design
3. ✓ All sections created:
   - Hero/About section with photo placeholder
   - Education section with examples
   - Projects showcase (3 projects)
   - Skills section with proficiency bars
   - Navigation and footer
4. ✓ Responsive design - Mobile, tablet, desktop friendly
5. ✓ Documentation - Complete README with customization guide

## Key Features

- **Tailwind CSS Framework**: Utility-first CSS with responsive design
- **Blue Theme**: Gradient blues and indigos throughout
- **Responsive Layout**: Works on all device sizes
- **No Build Process**: Uses Tailwind CDN, just open HTML file
- **Easy Customization**: Replace placeholder text with real information

## How to Use

1. Open `index.html` in your browser to view
2. For live editing:
   - Use VS Code Live Server extension
   - Or any local server
3. Replace placeholder content with your actual information
4. Customize colors by modifying Tailwind classes

## To Customize

### Your Information
- Replace "Your Name" with your actual name
- Update your profession/title
- Add your photo (replace image URL or use local file)
- Update education details
- Add your projects with descriptions
- Modify your skills and expertise areas

### Styling
- Colors: Modify blue/indigo/cyan classes to match your preference
- Fonts: Already optimized for readability
- Spacing: Adjust padding/margins via Tailwind utilities

## Deployment Options

1. **GitHub Pages** - Free hosting via GitHub
2. **Netlify** - Drag & drop deployment
3. **Vercel** - Automatic from GitHub
4. Any static hosting service

## File Structure

```
CV_PortfolioPersonal/
├── index.html              # Main portfolio page (single file)
├── README.md               # Documentation
└── .github/
    └── copilot-instructions.md  # This file
```

## Next Steps

1. Open `index.html` and view in browser
2. Update all placeholder text with your real information
3. Replace image URL with your professional photo
4. Customize colors if desired
5. Deploy to GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Vercel
